data.title: Starjoy Wellness and Travel Resets Use of IPO Proceeds, Extends Deployment to 2028
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Aoyuan Healthy Life Group Co. Ltd. ( (HK:3662) ).

Starjoy Wellness and Travel has reallocated and extended the timeline for using the remaining HK$136.4 million of net proceeds raised from its 2019 Hong Kong listing and subsequent over-allotment option, after having already deployed about RMB436.8 million mainly on acquisitions in commercial operational and property management services. The board has now earmarked RMB30 million of the unutilised funds for further acquisitions in those core service areas and RMB106.4 million for investments in property management, real estate asset operation and related lifecycle industries, targeting full utilisation by April 2028, signalling a continued push for scale and diversification within its operating portfolio.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3662) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.44 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Aoyuan Healthy Life Group Co. Ltd. stock, see the HK:3662 Stock Forecast page.

More about Aoyuan Healthy Life Group Co. Ltd.

Starjoy Wellness and Travel Company Limited, formerly Aoyuan Healthy Life Group Company Limited, operates in the commercial operational services and property management sector, with a focus on acquiring and investing in service providers in related and complementary areas. The Hong Kong-listed group also develops O2O platforms and intelligent service systems to support its property and commercial operations, and uses part of its capital for working capital and general corporate purposes.

An announcement from Winshine Science Co., Ltd. ( (HK:0209) ) is now available.

Winshine Science Company Limited has announced board changes effective 10 April 2026, with its governance framework continuing to feature a mix of executive and independent non-executive directors. The company’s current board lineup is led by chairlady and chief executive officer Wang Jingyu, supported by one other executive director and three independent non-executive directors.

Non-executive director Lin Shaopeng has resigned to devote more time to other business engagements and will step down from the nomination committee, with the company stating there is no disagreement or outstanding matter connected to his departure. Independent non-executive director Wu Yan Yee has been appointed to fill his seat on the nomination committee, a move that maintains committee continuity and signals the board’s intention to sustain robust oversight and succession planning processes.

data.description:

The latest update is out from Langham Hospitality Investments ( (HK:1270) ).

Langham Hospitality Investments announced that company secretary Marina Wong will resign from her roles as company secretary, authorised representative and process agent for both the trust manager and the listed company, effective 11 April 2026. The boards noted there is no disagreement or matter related to her departure that needs to be brought to the attention of the exchange or security holders, indicating an orderly transition.

The boards have appointed Anita Lee, a seasoned governance professional with more than 30 years of company secretarial and corporate governance experience, to assume all three roles from the same date. The move underscores the group’s emphasis on maintaining strong compliance and governance capabilities, and the boards publicly thanked Wong for her contributions while extending a formal welcome to Lee.

Langham Hospitality Investments and its associated trust operate in the hospitality and hotel investment sector, focusing on owning and managing hotel assets in Hong Kong. The structure combines a trust, managed by LHIL Manager Limited, and Langham Hospitality Investments Limited, which together provide investors exposure to hospitality real estate and related income streams in the region.

data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Industrial and Commercial Bank of China ( (HK:1398) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Industrial and Commercial Bank of China has completed the issuance of its 2026 Tier 2 Capital Notes (Series 1) via the National Interbank Bond Market under the Bond Connect scheme. The issue amounts to RMB50 billion of 10-year fixed-rate notes, carrying a 2.00% coupon and granting the bank a conditional redemption option at the end of year five.

Proceeds, after deducting issuance expenses, will be used to strengthen the bank’s Tier 2 capital in line with regulatory approvals and applicable laws. The transaction underscores ICBC’s continued focus on capital optimisation and regulatory capital adequacy, reinforcing its balance sheet resilience and supporting future business growth and risk-bearing capacity.

BOE Varitronix ( (HK:0710) ) has issued an announcement.

BOE Varitronix Limited has disclosed that its trustee purchased 204,000 shares on the market between 8 April and 10 April 2026 under its share award plan, representing about 0.026% of its issued share capital at an average price of HK$4.288 per share. The shares, costing a total of roughly HK$874,780, will be used to fund current and future grants under the long-term incentive scheme, which is designed to retain and attract key personnel, align employee and shareholder interests, and, according to the board, signals management’s confidence in the company’s prospects and financial strength while leaving future purchases subject to market conditions and board discretion.

The board emphasizes that the share award plan supports the group’s ongoing development by rewarding contributions to growth and improving competitiveness in its markets. By using market purchases instead of new issuance, the company aims to meet incentive needs without immediate dilution, while warning shareholders and potential investors that any further share purchases will depend on prevailing conditions and may vary in timing, size, and price.

SinoMedia Holding Limited has declared a final ordinary dividend of HKD 0.11 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with payment scheduled for 10 July 2026 following shareholder approval on 11 June 2026. data.title: Distinct Healthcare Moves Shareholder Communications Online Under New HKEX Rules
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited ( (HK:2677) ) has provided an update.

Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited has shifted to electronic dissemination of its corporate communications in line with amended Hong Kong listing rules effective 31 December 2023. Future documents such as annual and interim reports, meeting notices, listing documents and circulars will be published on the company’s website and the HKEX news platform in both English and Chinese, replacing routine distribution of printed copies.

The company will no longer send separate notifications when new documents are posted online, instead urging non-registered shareholders to monitor the websites proactively. Investors who still prefer hard copies can submit a request form or written request, with such elections remaining valid only until the end of the following financial year, signaling a clear push toward digital communications and reduced paper-based investor materials.

More about Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited

Distinct Healthcare Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2677. The group operates in the healthcare sector, providing medical and related clinical services, with its investor relations and regulatory disclosures focused on shareholders in the Hong Kong capital market.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Yue Yuen Industrial (Holdings) ( (HK:0551) ) has provided an announcement.

Yue Yuen Industrial (Holdings) reported unaudited net consolidated operating revenue of USD 604.0 million for March 2026 and USD 1.99 billion for the first three months of the year, reflecting year-on-year declines. Manufacturing revenue fell 11.1% in March and 5.5% year-to-date, while Pou Sheng’s revenue, measured in RMB, declined 6.3% for the month and 1.1% year-to-date, resulting in overall group revenue contractions of 8.2% for March and 2.2% year-to-date.

The announcement, aligned with disclosure obligations in Hong Kong and Taiwan due to Yue Yuen’s status as a PCC subsidiary, signals ongoing top-line pressure across both its manufacturing and China retail operations. The sustained revenue declines may indicate continued demand softness or competitive challenges in the footwear sector, with implications for margins, capacity utilization, and investor sentiment toward the group’s near-term performance.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0551) stock is a Sell with a HK$7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Yue Yuen Industrial (Holdings) stock, see the HK:0551 Stock Forecast page.

Around RMB90 million came from its survey, design and consultancy segment, while about RMB609 million was attributable to construction general contracting, underscoring the breadth of its integrated service capabilities.

Key wins include design and AI-enabled operation support services for Hangzhou Metro, digital industry and dispatching centers in Inner Mongolia, and multiple Beijing metro enhancement and safety assessment contracts. The group also captured a series of urban renewal, resettlement housing, campus construction and village redevelopment design mandates in Beijing and other regions, reinforcing its role in transport infrastructure and urban regeneration and supporting its future revenue pipeline.

data.title: Prudential Cancels Newly Repurchased Shares, Updates Voting Rights
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Prudential ( (GB:PRU) ) just unveiled an update.

Prudential plc has repurchased 251,388 of its ordinary shares of 5 pence each from JP Morgan Securities plc under an existing share buyback arrangement, at prices ranging between £11.0950 and £11.2950 and an average price of £11.1574. The company plans to cancel the repurchased shares, reducing the number of shares and voting rights in issue to 2,525,338,119, a move that tightens its capital base and may support earnings per share and shareholder value while aligning with regulatory requirements across its London and Hong Kong listings.

The shares were bought as on-exchange transactions on the London Stock Exchange and treated as on-market purchases under the Hong Kong Code on Share Buy-Backs, reflecting Prudential’s compliance with multi-jurisdictional market rules. The updated share count also provides a new reference point for investors assessing disclosure thresholds under UK transparency regulations, which may influence institutional reporting and ownership monitoring.

More about Prudential

Prudential plc is a UK-incorporated financial services group listed in London, Hong Kong and Singapore, focused on insurance and long-term savings. The company provides life and health insurance, retirement and investment products, with a strong presence in Asia and other growth markets, serving retail and institutional customers seeking protection and wealth management solutions.

Yield Go Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1796) ) has provided an announcement.

Metaspacex Limited has requested a halt in trading of its shares on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, effective from 9:00 a.m. on April 10, 2026. The suspension comes as the company prepares to issue an announcement regarding a reduction in the controlling shareholder’s stake, which has been classified as inside information and may signal a shift in ownership dynamics and governance considerations for investors.

The company has not yet disclosed detailed terms or reasons behind the controlling shareholder’s planned divestment, leaving the market awaiting clarity on potential changes in influence or strategic direction. The trading halt aims to ensure an orderly market and equal access to material information once the announcement is released, reflecting regulatory focus on transparency and investor protection in Hong Kong’s equity markets.
